description: Mapping between Rhena Rust constants, font-internal glyph names, and SMuFL cross-references for the Hildegard Neumes font. Order of `glyphs` is authoritative and defines Rhena's ALL_GLYPHS slice order. Do not reorder without coordinating with Rhena's `glyphs/mod.rs`.

glyphs.doc: Rhineland punctum: calligraphic parallelogram mark; basic single note.
Rhineland virga: thin vertical pen stroke with angular foot.
Small tilted diamond for climacus descending notes.
Wavy ornamental ascending note; 2–3 rounded calligraphic humps.
Ornamental repercussion note; small punctum body with a wavy tail. Directional split deferred.
Apostropha head; repeated by the renderer for bistropha and tristropha.
Rhineland pressus: vertical line attached to a short undulating line (Gardiner p. 94). No SMuFL equivalent.
Ascending liquescent terminal; small head + upward calligraphic tail. Lighter stroke per Gardiner p. 92.
Descending liquescent terminal; small head + downward calligraphic tail.
Small diminutive punctum for diminutive-liquescent positions.
Rhineland movable C clef: horizontal dash above a calligraphic C.
Rhineland F clef for low-range antiphons.
Short phrase break; thin bar spanning one staff space.
Medium phrase break; thin bar spanning the full 4-line staff.
Long phrase break; thin bar extending above and below the staff.
Final double bar; two equal-weight thin verticals.
Small tick breath mark above the staff.

Rhineland pes (integrated variant, interval of a 2nd): hook + short stem + head as one continuous calligraphic gesture. Rhena's resolver stamps this for pes-of-2nd; wider intervals compose rh_pes_foot + rh_pes_line + rh_pes_head. PUA U+F400 in the Rhineland non-SMuFL block.

Rhineland pes foot: bottom hook + small upward stem stub. Composed with rh_pes_line (stretched) + rh_pes_head by Rhena's resolver to form a pes-of-3rd-or-wider. Paleographic fidelity per ADR-0008 — the Hildegard pes bottom is a hook, not a punctum parallelogram. PUA U+F401.

Rhineland pes head: calligraphic head + rightward foot flick + small downward stem stub. No virga-style foot hook (that's a standalone-gesture terminator). Composed with rh_pes_foot + rh_pes_line by Rhena's resolver. PUA U+F402.
Ascending stroke connector for pes/scandicus joins. PUA U+F404 (skipping U+F403 which is PRESSUS).

Rhineland flexa (integrated variant, interval of a 2nd descending): head + short stem + foot as one continuous 'n'-shape calligraphic gesture. Rhena's resolver stamps this for flexa-of-2nd; wider intervals compose head+line+foot instead. PUA U+F405.

Rhineland flexa head: calligraphic head at the top of a descending flexa gesture + small downward stem stub. Composition partner for rh_flexa_line + rh_flexa_foot. Paleographically distinct from rh_pes_head (pen-entry stroke vs pen-exit stroke). PUA U+F406.

Rhineland flexa foot: small upward stem stub + foot hook terminator at the bottom of a descending flexa gesture. Composition partner for rh_flexa_head + rh_flexa_line. Paleographically distinct from rh_pes_foot (pen-exit terminator vs pen-entry hook). PUA U+F407.

Vertical stretchable connector for flexa compositions. PUA U+F408. Geometry: 38-du stem at x=[31, 69], 125 du tall extending from y=0 (top) to y=-125 (bottom). Rhena stretches vertically to fill the interval between rh_flexa_head's stem_bottom and rh_flexa_foot's stem_top (or substitutes a native SVG <line> at the stem_channel x-band per ADR-0012). 2026-04-20: redesigned from the abandoned-rhineland.rs diagonal parallelogram (172×260) after paleographic review of image 17 showed Hildegard's flexa stem is vertical, not diagonal.
